{"parentUuid":"cb9837b8-91ee-491c-a721-f38370d9bfb3","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"2dd4727c-ea1e-4329-9b10-a025b945bf6e","agentId":"ad8b14e39b6cb2d21","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019amooL2z5i1EPXpm2PM8RZ","type":"tool_result","content":"73	## §3 Design Pillars + tokens
74	
75	These are cross-cutting principles every WP in v3 references. Synthesised from R-A whitespace + cursor language, R-B AI-philosophy posture, R-G token-system audit, and R-D's anti-pattern catalog (the **negative reference**).
76	
77	### Pillars
78	
79	| ID  | Pillar                                                | One-line                                                                                                                       |
80	| --- | ----------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
81	| D1  | **Direct manipulation first, panels second**          | If a property has a visible representation, you should be able to drag it. Inspector is the secondary path, not primary.       |
82	| D2  | **Live preview always**                                | No drag, no input, no command should ever be type-and-pray. 60 fps preview before commit.                                       |
83	| D3  | **Density-conscious, but never crowded**               | Small typography, tight spacing, but every glyph carries meaning. No decorative chrome.                                        |
84	| D4  | **Color is information, not decoration**               | Palette serves discipline tints, advisory severity, drift, and category — never branding flourish.                              |
85	| D5  | **Motion conveys causality**                           | Every state change has an 80–240 ms motion arc that explains what happened. No teleporting elements.                              |
86	| D6  | **Comment & markup is first-class, not bolt-on**       | Anchor any comment to any element / region / sheet at any zoom level. Threadable. Resolvable. Never modal.                       |
87	| D7  | **The activity stream is the time machine**            | Every change is rewindable + restorable. Undo is the keyboard shortcut; the activity stream is the visual surface.               |
88	| D8  | **Context, not configuration**                         | Tools change behavior based on what's selected, what's hovered, what view is active. Less mode-switching, more sensing.            |
89	| D9  | **Mobile is parallel, not nested**                     | iPad sketch + viewer is its own surface — not a "responsive" version of desktop. Different jobs.                                  |
90	| D10 | **Output is a feature, not an afterthought**           | PDF / PPTX / web export is in the same code-paths as the canvas, not a separate exporter team. Brand templates are first-class.   |
91	
92	### Anti-pattern catalog (negative reference, from R-D §5)
93	
94	| Anti-pattern (R-D)                          | Pillar violated     | v3 antidote                                                          |
95	| ------------------------------------------- | ------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
96	| A1 Modal sketch sessions                     | D8                  | Soft contextual scopes (already shipped via SKT-01)                    |
97	| A2 Type vs Instance dichotomy in dialog      | D1                  | Single Inspector with "applies to: this / all 12" radio                 |
98	| A3 Ribbon-with-30-tabs                       | D3                  | Cmd palette + contextual panel                                          |
99	| A4 Dialog-for-everything                     | D1                  | Inspector + drawer overlay                                              |
100	| A5 Tooltip-as-documentation                   | D3                  | Text labels at panel widths ≥ 240 px                                    |
101	| A6 Worksets / central-file vocab              | D7                  | Figma-grade vocab; activity stream as time machine                       |
102	| A7 Phasing dual-dropdown                     | D8                  | Single "view shows: existing / demolition / new" dropdown bound to view  |
103	| A8 VG matrix dialog (~120 toggles)           | D8                  | Discipline lens + view template + view filter + right-click on a category |
104	| A9 Always-visible Properties panel           | D1                  | Inspector appears only on selection; empty-state is the model itself     |
105	| A10 "Project Browser blue" engineering palette | D4                | Warm ochre accent + drafting palette + R-G phased adoption                |
106	
107	### Token reference (full proposal in `spec/v3-research/design-tokens.md`)
108	
109	The v3 token set is 3-layer-separated:
110	
111	- **Layer A — Structural (always ours).** `--space-*`, `--radius-*`, `--text-*`, `--motion-*`, `--ease-*`, `--draft-*`, `--cat-*`, `--disc-*`, `--color-{success,warning,danger,info,drift}`. Never themed.
112	- **Layer B — Semantic Shell (light / dark / customer).** `--color-{background,foreground,surface*,border*,muted-foreground,ring}`. Swap between light, dark, and customer-tinted variants without touching brand.
113	- **Layer C — Brand (re-themable to customer's CI).** `--brand-accent`, `--brand-accent-fg`, `--brand-logo-mark`, `--brand-typeface`. A customer's CI is a single CSS file. **Brand-swap CI invariant test** asserts only Layer C resolves change when the brand is swapped — drafting palette, line weights, motion stay byte-identical.
114	
115	**Headline token shifts in v3:**
116	
117	- Chrome accent `hsl(214 88% 50%)` cobalt → `#b8763a` warm ochre (light) / `#d9924a` (dark). The single biggest "feels engineering" tell, replaced.
118	- Discipline tints: `--disc-arch` sage `#6e8a72`, `--disc-struct` taupe `#8b6f57`, `--disc-mep` slate-blue `#5b7a8c`. Each with a `-soft` variant via `color-mix`. Feeds T8.
119	- New `--color-drift` (light `#9b6f1b` / dark `#d2a256`) — first-class severity, separates drift from warning.
120	- New `--text-2xs` (10 px / 14 px) — legitimises the canvas-overlay regime where 627 `text-[Npx]` escapes live today.
121	- New `--ease-paper` cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1) — slow-finish for commit/settle moments. Reads as ink hitting paper.
122	- New `--radius-canvas: 0` — sidebars, rails, status bar, plan / 3D / sheet canvases never get radius. Codifies the "sidebars are sidebars, not cards" rule.
123	
124	Adoption is phased: introduce `tokens-v3.css` alongside v1 (no component changes), then migrate by feature folder (workspace → plan → viewport → families → tools), then deprecate hex literals via an ESLint rule.
125	
126	### Rendering-quality opinions (canvas-side, the marquee surface)
127	
128	**Plan canvas (the single biggest design-quality lever):**
129	
130	- Cut lines heavy, projection fine, witness hairline. Hatches scale with paper-mm at the active plot scale, not screen pixels. Dimension typography in tabular nums at `--text-2xs`. Witness lines `--draft-witness` at `--draft-lw-witness`. The padlock glyph from R-A (associative-snap acquired) is implemented as a 12 × 12 px overlay near the cursor when `snapEngine` returns an associative match.
131	- Line weights at zoom levels: at 1:50 plot scale, cut lines are 2 px (major) / 1.4 px (minor); at 1:100, 70 %; at 1:200, 50 %; at 1:500, 0.4 px cut + projection suppressed.
132	- Grid: major at zoom > 1:200 only; minor at zoom > 1:100 only.
133	
134	**3D viewport:**
135	
136	- Background is a linear vertical gradient, `--draft-paper` at horizon to slightly darker at top. Avoid pure white (studio-photo) and pure grey (engineering-drab).
137	- Sun at 35° elevation, 145° azimuth — north-east-by-east, a real architectural sun position.
138	- Ochre selection halo at 2 px stroke + 0.4 opacity glow. Never blue. Never a thick rectangle.
139	- SSAO tuned toward "drawn shadow," not "rendered shadow" — short occlusion radius, gentle falloff.
140	- Paper-grain noise on wall / floor materials at low intensity so surfaces don't read as plastic.
141	
142	These canvas decisions are tracked as WPs inside T2 and T4. Token tables alone don't make a product feel architectural — canvas rendering with token-driven discipline does.
143	
144	---
145	
146	## §UX Workflow Design — chrome layout, top bar, role-based workspaces, tool grammar
147	
148	### Why this section
149	
150	The nine themes describe **what bim-ai does**; this section describes **the chrome it does it inside** — top bar, side rails, status bar, command grammar, workspace switcher. Revit's chrome is the counter-example: a 12-tab ribbon (A3), an always-visible 320 px Properties panel (A9), a 120-toggle VG matrix dialog (A8), icon-only buttons that depend on tooltips for documentation (A5), and a "Project Browser blue" palette (A10). Every one of those is a chrome decision; every one is what we are not building.
151	
152	The bet is that **good chrome is invisible**. Where Revit asks "what dialog do I open?" bim-ai answers "the rails appear on selection; the dropdown lives where you'd expect; `Cmd+K` covers everything else." T1's phasing dropdown lives in the view header, T3's activity stream behind the top-bar entry, T8's lens in the status bar, T9's agent panel slides in from the right. Get the chrome right and themes inherit; get it wrong and every theme fights the chassis.
153

414	### Section takeaway
415	
416	Chrome that gets out of the way is the second-largest design-quality lever after canvas rendering quality. A top bar that holds identity not tools, a status bar that holds the lens not a 120-toggle dialog, an Inspector that appears only on selection, a `Cmd+K` palette that absorbs every long-tail action, a tool grammar uniform across every authoring verb, and discipline-tinted chrome that signals workspace at a glance — together these make a young architect say "this feels architectural, not engineering" in the first thirty seconds. **Chrome and canvas are 90 % of the "feels architectural" bet.**

3060:#### API-V3-01 — Tool / CLI surface contract (the keystone)
3062:_Source: §L Locked Decisions (Agent / software boundary) carried to its logical conclusion; user reframe 2026-05-08 ("bim-ai will be AI-native, in that it will be 100 % usable via AI agents directly"); Claude Code's MCP pattern as design vocabulary. **Without API-V3-01, the rest of T9 is fiction** — there is no agent-callable surface, just a kernel some humans drive through a UI._
3130:**Engine.** New module `app/bim_ai/tkn/` with `encode(elements) → TokenSequence` (deterministic order), `decode(seq, kernel_state) → list[Command]`, `validate(seq) → list[Advisory]`, `diff(a, b) → TokenSequenceDelta`. Wired into `try_commit_bundle`: every bundle round-trips through `encode → decode → encode` and produces a deep-equal sequence (modulo float epsilon). CLI exposes `bim-ai tokens encode | decode | diff` (registered as tool descriptors per API-V3-01).
3134:**Cross-theme references.** **API-V3-01 (every TKN verb is a registered tool).** **T1 (every kernel kind exports `tokenize()` / `untokenize()`; KRN-V3-04 Design Options live inside the token schema).** **T5 (catalogKey resolves to T5 instances).** **IMG-V3-01 (deterministic CV emits TokenSequence as wire format).**
3170:**Engine.** New module `app/bim_ai/cmd/` with `apply_bundle(bundle, mode) → BundleResult`. Wraps kernel command application with the validation + assumption + revision contract. CLI: `bim-ai apply-bundle <bundle.json> --base <revision> [--dry-run | --commit]`. REST: `POST /api/v3/bundles`. Both registered per API-V3-01. Idempotent: same bundle replayed against the same parent yields the same new revision.
3174:**Cross-theme references.** **API-V3-01.** **T1 KRN-V3-04 (every commit lands as an option per OPT-V3-01).** **T5 (catalogKey resolution at apply time).** **CQ-04 (`engine.py` split must complete first).**
3200:**Cross-theme references.** **API-V3-01.** **CMD-V3-01.** **T3 (activity stream renders assumption entries).** **§L (operational instrument of the locked decision).**
3214:**Cross-theme references.** **API-V3-01.** **CMD-V3-01.** **T1 KRN-V3-04 (load-bearing dependency — primitive lives there).** **T3 (activity stream renders option-creation events).**
3237:**Engine.** Server-side queue (single-process for v3.0; pluggable later). Endpoints (registered per API-V3-01): `POST /jobs`, `GET /jobs?modelId=X`, `POST /jobs/:id/cancel | retry`. Status broadcast via existing WebSocket. **CQ-01 hard prerequisite** — without reliable broadcast, the panel can show stale state.
3241:**Cross-theme references.** **CQ-01 (hard prerequisite).** **API-V3-01.** **T2 (Jobs panel toggle is part of the U7 view-as-lens chrome).** **EXP-V3-01 (export jobs flow here).** **IMG-V3-01 (sketch-trace runs as a job for large images).**
3274:**Engine.** New CLI: `bim-ai trace --image <path> [--brief <path>] [--archetype-hint <key>] -o layout.json`. Runs SKB-04 + SKB-14 + SKB-07 (deterministic) + polygon recovery + optional OCR. Output: `(StructuredLayout, advisories)`. Registered as a tool descriptor per API-V3-01. The brief is passed through as metadata only — the deterministic CV does not interpret it; agents that read the brief do their own thing with it.
3278:**Cross-theme references.** **API-V3-01 (registered tool).** **TKN-V3-01 (output JSON is round-trippable into envelope tokens).** **T

425:4. **§7 Code Quality** is a parallel track of architectural debt that rides alongside theme work.
1233:**Why it matters.** T3 is the wedge for **B3 Live Collaboration with Time Machine** — the Figma-meets-Bluebeam bet. Revit's collaboration vocabulary (worksets, central files, "synchronize with central") is Subversion-era and reads as A6 in our anti-pattern catalog (R-D §5). Motif's deepest pillar today is collaboration semantics, not authoring (R-B §6) — Motif is "unambiguously deeper" than Rayon there, and that is the area where v3-T3 should learn most from Motif. Closing T3 unlocks the whole B3 felt-outcome — two architects co-author the model live, a third reviewer leaves twelve 3D-anchored comments on a sheet, and the activity stream is a real time machine. T3 is also the surface that pays back **D6 Comment & markup is first-class** and **D7 Activity stream is the time machine** — the two pillars that distinguish v3 from every Revit-clone in the market. Without T3, B3 is unbuildable; without B3, the small-practice studio that picked bim-ai for B1 still has to ship reviews via marked-up PDFs and email. **CQ-01 (WebSocket robustness) is a hard prerequisite** — unreliable WS = unreliable multi-user; the v3 tracker §7 calls this out explicitly. T3's distinctive vibe is **async-first + spatially-anchored**, deliberately not live-cursor-voice (which lives in V-06).
1273:**Scope.** **Hard prerequisite: CQ-01 WebSocket robustness must complete before this WP ships** — unreliable WS = silent state divergence in multi-user sessions; the v3 tracker §7 calls this out explicitly. Pick **yjs** (CRDT) over WS+OT for v3 — yjs is the production-grade web CRDT, integrates with Y-WebSocket on the server, supports awareness (presence) as a side-channel, and naturally extends to offline-tolerant authoring (COL-V3-06). The v2 architecture is server-authoritative on commit with broadcast via websocket and per-user undo stacks (per §O OpenBIM Stance) — yjs slots in as the in-flight transport between commits without changing the snapshot model. Each kernel command becomes a yjs Y.Doc transaction; the server is still the authority that resolves the canonical snapshot at every commit boundary.
1302:**Cross-theme references.** **Hard dep: CQ-01.** T2 (selection halos visible to others reuse the EDT-01 halo protocol with cross-user color); T9 (agent edits flow through the same yjs commit stream — agent operations look like another participant); §7 CQ-04 (Python god-file split affects the kernel's ability to ingest concurrent command streams cleanly).

## Merged so far

| WP-ID | Branch | Merged at | Tracker status |
| ----- | ------ | --------- | -------------- |
| (none yet) | | | |

## Held back (deps not ready)

- **KRN-V3-02 stacked walls** — heavy `engine.py` overlap with KRN-V3-01 (WP-004); dispatch after WP-004 merges.
- **KRN-V3-03 multi-roof + fascia/gutter + soffit** — heavy `engine.py` overlap with KRN-V3-01; dispatch after WP-004 merges.
- **KRN-V3-05 stair-by-sketch** — `engine.py` overlap; needs clean kernel after KRN-V3-01 lands.
- **CMD-V3-01 command-bundle apply** — depends on API-V3-01 (WP-003) + TKN-V3-01 (WP-005); dispatch after both merge.
- **CQ-01 WebSocket robustness** — non-trivial; schedule after Sprint 0 lands (WP-001 unblocks momentum).
- **CQ-04 god-file split** — high merge-conflict risk against ANY in-flight T1/T9 work; schedule for quiet window between bet-cycles.
- **CQ-03 workspace/ reorg** — riskiest CQ item; dedicated quiet week needed; WSP-V3-01 (T8) has soft prereq.
- **All CHR-V3-* chrome WPs** — wait for Sprint 0 (WP-001) merge so they inherit v3 tokens automatically.
- **COL-V3-01 multi-user** — hard deps: CQ-01 must land first.

## File-overlap notes

- WP-004 (KRN-V3-01 phasing) and WP-006 (KRN-V3-06 plan region) both touch `app/bim_ai/engine.py` in different sub-areas (phasing vs view_range). Low conflict risk but watch on merge — dispatch WP-006 only after confirming WP-004's exact engine.py changes.

- WP-007 (SUN-V3-01) touches `packages/web/src/viewport/` + small `engine.py` (SunSettings). Disjoint from WP-004/006 sub-areas.
- WP-008 (CQ-02) touches `app/pyproject.toml` + new `app/uv.lock` + `Makefile` + CI yaml. Zero overlap with everything else.
- CQ-04 god-file split is a known merge-conflict magnet vs every in-flight T1 / T9 WP; schedule between bet-cycles only.

## Notes

- Sprint 0 (WP-001) MUST merge before any CHR-V3-* or new-component WP starts.
- CQ-01 is a hard prereq for COL-V3-01 (T3) + JOB-V3-01 + OUT-V3-01.
- API-V3-01 (WP-003) cross-cuts every kernel WP per §U — every WP that lands a new kernel verb must add CLI + REST/RPC + JSON schema + assumption-log per the API-V3-01 contract once that WP is merged.

- Concurrency cap: 8 heavy workers in flight at any time.

- **KRN-V3-03 multi-roof + fascia/gutter + soffit** — heavy `engine.py` overlap with KRN-V3-01; dispatch after WP-004 merges.
- **KRN-V3-05 stair-by-sketch** — `engine.py` overlap; needs clean kernel after KRN-V3-01 lands.

- WP-004 (KRN-V3-01 phasing) and WP-006 (KRN-V3-06 plan region) both touch `app/bim_ai/engine.py` in different sub-areas (phasing vs view_range). Low conflict risk but watch on merge — dispatch WP-006 only after confirming WP-004's exact engine.py changes.
- KRN-V3-02 stacked walls + KRN-V3-03 multi-roof held back to avoid triple `engine.py` overlap with WP-004; dispatch after WP-004 merges.
- WP-003 (API-V3-01) touches `packages/cli/cli.mjs` + new `app/bim_ai/api/` (mostly new). Low overlap with others.
- WP-005 (TKN-V3-01) is mostly green-field in new `app/bim_ai/tkn/` module; thin `engine.py` integration.
- WP-007 (SUN-V3-01) touches `packages/web/src/viewport/` + small `engine.py` (SunSettings). Disjoint from WP-004/006 sub-areas.
- WP-008 (CQ-02) touches `app/pyproject.toml` + new `app/uv.lock` + `Makefile` + CI yaml. Zero overlap with everything else.
- CQ-04 god-file split is a known merge-conflict magnet vs every in-flight T1 / T9 WP; schedule between bet-cycles only.

## Notes

- Sprint 0 (WP-001) MUST merge before any CHR-V3-* or new-component WP starts.
- CQ-01 is a hard prereq for COL-V3-01 (T3) + JOB-V3-01 + OUT-V3-01.
- API-V3-01 (WP-003) cross-cuts every kernel WP per §U — every WP that lands a new kernel verb must add CLI + REST/RPC + JSON schema + assumption-log per the API-V3-01 contract once that WP is merged.
- TKN-V3-01 (WP-005) is a gating keystone for CMD-V3-01, OPT-V3-01, IMG-V3-01 — dispatch those only after WP-005 merges.
- Concurrency cap: 8 heavy workers in flight at any time.
- WP-004 and WP-005 are both XL/L effort — they will be in flight simultaneously. Watch for `engine.py` conflicts if both are active; they touch different sub-areas.

- **CQ-04 god-file split** — high merge-conflict risk against ANY in-flight T1/T9 work; schedule for quiet window between bet-cycles.
- **CQ-03 workspace/ reorg** — riskiest CQ item; dedicated quiet week needed; WSP-V3-01 (T8) has soft prereq.
- **All CHR-V3-* chrome WPs** — wait for Sprint 0 (WP-001) merge so they inherit v3 tokens automatically.
- **COL-V3-01 multi-user** — hard deps: CQ-01 must land first.

## File-overlap notes

- WP-004 (KRN-V3-01 phasing) and WP-006 (KRN-V3-06 plan region) both touch `app/bim_ai/engine.py` in different sub-areas (phasing vs view_range). Low conflict risk but watch on merge — dispatch WP-006 only after confirming WP-004's exact engine.py changes.
- KRN-V3-02 stacked walls + KRN-V3-03 multi-roof held back to avoid triple `engine.py` overlap with WP-004; dispatch after WP-004 merges.
- WP-003 (API-V3-01) touches `packages/cli/cli.mjs` + new `app/bim_ai/api/` (mostly new). Low overlap with others.
- WP-005 (TKN-V3-01) is mostly green-field in new `app/bim_ai/tkn/` module; thin `engine.py` integration.
- WP-007 (SUN-V3-01) touches `packages/web/src/viewport/` + small `engine.py` (SunSettings). Disjoint from WP-004/006 sub-areas.
- WP-008 (CQ-02) touches `app/pyproject.toml` + new `app/uv.lock` + `Makefile` + CI yaml. Zero overlap with everything else.
- CQ-04 god-file split is a known merge-conflict magnet vs every in-flight T1 / T9 WP; schedule between bet-cycles only.

## Notes

- Sprint 0 (WP-001) MUST merge before any CHR-V3-* or new-component WP starts.
- CQ-01 is a hard prereq for COL-V3-01 (T3) + JOB-V3-01 + OUT-V3-01.
- API-V3-01 (WP-003) cross-cuts every kernel WP per §U — every WP that lands a new kernel verb must add CLI + REST/RPC + JSON schema + assumption-log per the API-V3-01 contract once that WP is merged.
- TKN-V3-01 (WP-005) is a gating keystone for CMD-V3-01, OPT-V3-01, IMG-V3-01 — dispatch those only after WP-005 merges.
- Concurrency cap: 8 heavy workers in flight at any time.
- WP-004 and WP-005 are both XL/L effort — they will be in flight simultaneously. Watch for `engine.py` conflicts if both are active; they touch different sub-areas.

    feat(tkn): tokenised kernel representation — encode/decode/validate/diff
    
    Introduces app/bim_ai/tkn/ (pure-function token layer) with:
    - encode: elements → deterministic TokenSequence
    - decode: TokenSequence × kernel_state → commands
    - validate: structural advisory checks (orphan host, out-of-range)
    - diff: structural delta between two TokenSequences

    TokenSequenceDelta) added to packages/core. CLI bim-ai tokens
    encode|decode|diff registered per WP-005 API-V3-01. MoveElementCmd
    added to commands + engine handler. Round-trip wired into
    try_commit_bundle as a determinism gate. 24 acceptance tests green.

content.text:
Good — 6 of 8 WPs have real content. Two issues to flag upfront:
- **WP-002 (EDT)**: branch exists but 0 commits — **not implemented**
- **WP-007 (SUN)**: commit `ab34882d` landed on the phasing branch (`feat/v3-krn-v3-01-phasing`) instead of its own branch, but the work is complete and solid

All other 6 WPs look well-scoped with tests. Several branches have extra seed/viewport commits mixed in from parallel sessions — I'll cherry-pick just the WP commits rather than doing full branch merges to keep main clean.

Let me check the base state and run validation before touching main.

=== WP-004 commands.py diff (first 40 lines) ===
commit 31cae0bb55dd11678ba19eb36d9d4bcd0ad20404
Author: Johannes Hötter <jhoetter@MacBook-Pro-von-Johannes.local>
Date:   Fri May 8 16:24:40 2026 +0200

    feat(kernel): KRN-V3-01 phasing primitive
    
    Introduces the Phase element kind and phasing fields across the BIM
    kernel — Python, TypeScript, and CLI layers — per spec/v3-prompts/wp-004.
    
    Added:
    - PhaseElem (kind: "phase", ord, name) to elements.py + TS index.ts
    - phase_created / phase_demolished fields on 16 physical element classes
    - phase_filter field (PhaseFilter literal) on PlanViewElem
    - 7 new Pydantic commands: CreatePhase, RenamePhase, ReorderPhase,
      DeletePhase, SetElementPhase, SetViewPhase, SetViewPhaseFilter
    - 7 match cases in engine.apply_inplace with ord-collision and
      reference-integrity guards
    - try_commit_bundle now catches ValueError/KeyError from apply_inplace
      and Pydantic ValidationError from coerce_command, returning (False,...)
    - 7 CLI handlers in cli.mjs (phase-create, phase-rename, phase-reorder,
      phase-delete, element-set-phase, view-set-phase, view-set-phase-filter)
    - 20 tests in app/tests/test_phasing_primitive.py (all pass)
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    Entire-Checkpoint: 46163be531b7

diff --git a/app/bim_ai/commands.py b/app/bim_ai/commands.py
index 27ce9a87..9ab814f3 100644
--- a/app/bim_ai/commands.py
+++ b/app/bim_ai/commands.py
@@ -13,6 +13,7 @@ from bim_ai.elements import (
     DormerRoofKind,
     DxfLineworkPrim,
     EvidenceRef,
+    PhaseFilter,
     PlanCategoryGraphicRow,
     PlanTagBadgeStyle,
     PlanTagTarget,
@@ -1048,6 +1049,46 @@ class MoveSurveyPointCmd(BaseModel):
     shared_elevation_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="sharedElevationMm")
 
 
+# --- SUN-V3-01: sun_settings commands -------------------------------------------
+
+
+class CreateSunSettingsCmd(BaseModel):
+    """SUN-V3-01: create the project-level sun settings singleton.
+
+    Rejects if one already exists (use UpdateSunSettings to modify).
+    """
+
+    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
+    type: Literal["createSunSettings"] = "createSunSettings"
+    id: str | None = None
+    latitude_deg: float = Field(default=48.13, alias="latitudeDeg")
+    longitude_deg: float = Field(default=11.58, alias="longitudeDeg")
+    date_iso: str = Field(default="2026-06-21", alias="dateIso")
+    time_of_day: dict = Field(
+        default_factory=lambda: {"hours": 14, "minutes": 30}, alias="timeOfDay"
+    )

=== WP-005 commands.py diff ===
commit 14c2fd6e283c137de5564862864ca89e933c1413
Author: Johannes Hötter <jhoetter@MacBook-Pro-von-Johannes.local>
Date:   Fri May 8 16:06:47 2026 +0200

    feat(tkn): tokenised kernel representation — encode/decode/validate/diff
    
    Introduces app/bim_ai/tkn/ (pure-function token layer) with:
    - encode: elements → deterministic TokenSequence
    - decode: TokenSequence × kernel_state → commands
    - validate: structural advisory checks (orphan host, out-of-range)
    - diff: structural delta between two TokenSequences
    
    TypeScript types (EntityToken, EnvelopeToken, TokenSequence,
    TokenSequenceDelta) added to packages/core. CLI bim-ai tokens
    encode|decode|diff registered per WP-005 API-V3-01. MoveElementCmd
    added to commands + engine handler. Round-trip wired into
    try_commit_bundle as a determinism gate. 24 acceptance tests green.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    Entire-Checkpoint: ad96a624d3c0

+++ b/app/bim_ai/commands.py
@@ -1545,6 +1545,15 @@ class CreateConstraintCmd(BaseModel):
     severity: Literal["warning", "error"] = "error"
 
 
+class MoveElementCmd(BaseModel):
+    """TKN-V3-01 — move a wall-hosted element (door/window) to a new tAlongHost position."""
+
+    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
+    type: Literal["moveElement"] = "moveElement"
+    element_id: str = Field(alias="elementId")
+    t_along_host: float = Field(alias="tAlongHost", ge=0.0, le=1.0)
+
+
 Command = Annotated[
     CreateLevelCmd

commit 30c7e3cc29670f39138784fe27209676b0f31397
Author: Johannes Hötter <jhoetter@MacBook-Pro-von-Johannes.local>
Date:   Fri May 8 16:11:32 2026 +0200

    feat(kernel): KRN-V3-06 — plan region cut-plane override
    
    Implements WP-006: sub-rectangle in a plan view with a different
    cut-plane height than the parent view.
    
    Python engine:
    - UpdatePlanRegionCmd + DeletePlanRegionCmd (commands.py)
    - Dispatch cases in engine.py (update supports name, outline, cut-plane;
      delete validates element kind before removal)
    
    TypeScript:
    - extractPlanRegionOverlays + pointInPolygonMm in planProjection.ts
    - PlanRegionOverlay type export
    - plan-region in PlanTool (storeTypes.ts), ToolId + registry entry
      (toolRegistry.ts), KNOWN_PLAN_TOOLS + LegacyPlanTool (workspaceUtils.ts)
    - PlanCanvas: import, Draft union variant, KRN-V3-06 dashed boundary
      rendering via --draft-witness CSS token, two-click tool handler
    - InspectorPlanRegionEditor component in InspectorContent.tsx
    
    CLI:
    - plan-region create/update/delete subcommands in cli.mjs
    
    Tests:
    - 11 Python engine tests (test_plan_region_commands.py)
    - 8 TypeScript unit tests (planProjection.planRegion.test.ts)
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    Entire-Checkpoint: a3d14173ba43

@@ -684,6 +684,20 @@ class CreatePlanRegionCmd(BaseModel):
     outline_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="outlineMm")
     cut_plane_offset_mm: float = Field(alias="cutPlaneOffsetMm", default=-500)

content.content:
+
+
+class DeletePhaseCmd(BaseModel):
+    """KRN-V3-01 — delete a phase, optionally retargeting its elements."""
+
+    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
+    type: Literal["deletePhase"] = "deletePhase"
+    phase_id: str = Field(alias="phaseId")
+    retarget_to_phase_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="retargetToPhaseId")
+
+
+class SetElementPhaseCmd(BaseModel):

    if (cmd === 'tokens') {
      const sub = argv[0];
      if (sub === 'encode') {
        if (!modelId) usage();
        const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/models/${modelId}/tokens/encode`, {
          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        });
        if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`tokens encode: ${res.status} ${await res.text()}`);
        console.log(JSON.stringify(await res.json(), null, 2));
        return;
      }
      if (sub === 'decode') {
        if (!modelId) usage();
        const filePath = argv[1];
        let seqJson;
        if (!filePath || filePath === '-') {
          const chunks = [];
          for await (const chunk of process.stdin) chunks.push(chunk);
          seqJson = JSON.parse(Buffer.concat(chunks).toString());
        } else {
          const { readFileSync } = await import('fs');
          seqJson = JSON.parse(readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'));
        }
        const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/models/${modelId}/tokens/decode`, {
          method: 'POST',
          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
          body: JSON.stringify({ sequence: seqJson }),
        });
        if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`tokens decode: ${res.status} ${await res.text()}`);
        console.log(JSON.stringify(await res.json(), null, 2));
        return;
      }
      if (sub === 'diff') {
        if (!modelId) usage();
        const aPath = argv.find((_, i) => argv[i - 1] === '--a');
        const bPath = argv.find((_, i) => argv[i - 1] === '--b');
        if (!aPath || !bPath) {
          console.error('Usage: tokens diff --a <path> --b <path>');
          process.exit(1);
        }
        const { readFileSync } = await import('fs');
        const seqA = JSON.parse(readFileSync(aPath, 'utf8'));
        const seqB = JSON.parse(readFileSync(bPath, 'utf8'));
        const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/models/${modelId}/tokens/diff`, {
          method: 'POST',
          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
          body: JSON.stringify({ sequenceA: seqA, sequenceB: seqB }),
        });
        if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`tokens diff: ${res.status} ${await res.text()}`);
        console.log(JSON.stringify(await res.json(), null, 2));
        return;
      }
      console.error(`Unknown tokens subcommand: ${sub ?? '(none)'}. Use encode, decode, or diff.`);
=======
    if (cmd === 'plan-region') {
      const sub = argv[0];
      if (sub === 'create') {
        if (!modelId) usage();
        let levelId, cutPlaneOffsetMm, name, coords;
        const rest = argv.slice(1);
        for (let i = 0; i < rest.length; i++) {
          if (rest[i] === '--level') levelId = rest[++i];
          else if (rest[i] === '--cut') cutPlaneOffsetMm = Number(rest[++i]);
          else if (rest[i] === '--name') name = rest[++i];
          else coords = rest.slice(i);
        }
        if (!levelId || !coords || coords.length < 4) {
          console.error('plan-region create: --level <id> x0 y0 x1 y1 required');
          process.exit(1);
        }
        const [x0, y0, x1, y1] = coords.map(Number);
        const cmd = {
          type: 'createPlanRegion',
          levelId,
          outlineMm: [
            { xMm: x0, yMm: y0 },
            { xMm: x1, yMm: y0 },
            { xMm: x1, yMm: y1 },
            { xMm: x0, yMm: y1 },
          ],
          ...(cutPlaneOffsetMm !== undefined ? { cutPlaneOffsetMm } : {}),
          ...(name !== undefined ? { name } : {}),
        };
        await commit(modelId, cmd);
        return;
      }
      if (sub === 'update') {
        if (!modelId) usage();
        const id = argv[1];
        if (!id) { console.error('plan-region update: <id> required'); process.exit(1); }
        const rest = argv.slice(2);
        const updates = { type: 'updatePlanRegion', id };
        for (let i = 0; i < rest.length; i++) {
          if (rest[i] === '--cut') updates.cutPlaneOffsetMm = Number(rest[++i]);
          else if (rest[i] === '--name') updates.name = rest[++i];
        }
        await commit(modelId, updates);
        return;
      }
      if (sub === 'delete') {
        if (!modelId) usage();
        const id = argv[1];
        if (!id) { console.error('plan-region delete: <id> required'); process.exit(1); }
        await commit(modelId, { type: 'deletePlanRegion', id });
        return;
      }
      console.error(`Unknown plan-region subcommand: ${sub ?? '(none)'}. Use create, update, or delete.`);

content.content:
43:**WPs bundled.** API-V3-01 (tool/CLI surface contract — keystone) → TKN-V3-01 (tokenised representation) → CMD-V3-01 (command-bundle apply API) → IMG-V3-01 (image → structured layout — deterministic CV today, AI-swappable later) → CMD-V3-02 (assumption log primitive) → JOB-V3-01 (Jobs panel for long-running ops) → OPT-V3-01 (Design Options as scratch) → VG-V3-01 (render-and-compare CLI tool) → CTL-V3-01 (catalog query API) → OUT-V3-01 (live web link) + OUT-V3-02 (Frames + Views to PPTX). The actual AI rendering integration (RND-V3-01) is `vision`.
52:**WPs bundled.** Multi-user editing on yjs/WebSocket (with **CQ-01 WS-robustness** as hard prerequisite) → 3D-anchored comments + threading + @mention → Markups on plan + 3D + sheet (Rayon `AN` pattern + freehand surface markup) → Activity stream as time-machine (Linear-grade history, Rayon's snapshot model with named milestones) → permission tiers (admin / editor / viewer / public-link viewer) → Designer's Bill of Rights as customer-facing artifact (R-B copyable).
54:**Critical path estimate.** 3 calendar months. CQ-01 must complete before T3 ships.
66:Plus cross-cutting: Designer's Bill of Rights + monthly essay program (R-B brand layer); chrome discipline tinting (LNS-V3-02 + CHR-V3-09).
261:A v2 EDT-06 follow-through: today the modifiers are scattered per-tool. CHR-V3-08 codifies them in a single `<ToolModifierBar>` mounted by every authoring tool. **The same vocabulary works in every tool** — consistency by construction.
314:#### CHR-V3-01 — Top-bar component
324:#### CHR-V3-02 — Workspace switcher
334:#### CHR-V3-03 — Status bar with lens dropdown
344:#### CHR-V3-04 — Cmd+K command palette
354:#### CHR-V3-05 — Activity-stream entry + drawer
364:#### CHR-V3-06 — Right-rail Inspector behaviour
374:#### CHR-V3-07 — Left-rail Project Browser refresh
384:#### CHR-V3-08 — Secondary contextual bar
394:#### CHR-V3-09 — Discipline-tinted chrome via `--disc-*` tokens
404:#### CHR-V3-10 — Empty-state canvas (Inspector hidden)
499:| KRN-V3-02 | Stacked walls | M | now | — |
500:| KRN-V3-03 | Multi-roof composition + fascia/gutter + soffit (G11+G12+G13 bundled) | XL | now | — |
502:| KRN-V3-05 | Stair by sketch | L | now | — |
504:| KRN-V3-07 | Slanted & tapered walls | M | next | KRN-V3-02 |
505:| KRN-V3-08 | Wall sweeps & reveals | M | next | KRN-V3-03 (edge-profile-run pattern) |
507:| KRN-V3-10 | Monolithic / floating stair sub-kinds | M | next | KRN-V3-05 |
508:| KRN-V3-11 | Railing baluster pattern + handrail supports | M | next | KRN-V3-05 |
548:#### KRN-V3-02 — Stacked walls
573:#### KRN-V3-03 — Multi-roof composition + fascia/gutter + soffit (bundled)
646:#### KRN-V3-05 — Stair by sketch
720:**Scope.** Horizontal profile run along a wall (sweep — additive, e.g. cornice / water-table / plinth) or inverse cut (reveal). Reuses the `EdgeProfileRun` infra shipped in KRN-V3-03 — wall sweeps are just edge-profile-runs hosted to a wall edge instead of a roof edge. Auto-mitred at corners.
721:**Data model.** Reuses `EdgeProfileRun` (KRN-V3-03 shape) with `hostElementId` pointing at a wall and `hostEdge: 'top' | 'bottom' | { startMm; endMm }`. Adds an inverse-cut variant via `mode: 'sweep' | 'reveal'`.
723:**UI.** "Edge Profile" tool (already shipped in KRN-V3-03) accepts a wall edge as host. Mode toggle: sweep / reveal.
725:**Effort.** M — 1 week (most plumbing already in KRN-V3-03; just hosts onto walls + reveal mode).
862:**Engine.** Each sketch-mode element kind exports a `gripProvider` that yields vertex-grips + edge-mid-grips. Drag commits via existing `MoveSketchVertex` command. Ties into KRN-V3-05 (stair-by-sketch boundary editing).
894:**Theme takeaway.** Shipping T1 turns bim-ai from a residential-MVP authoring tool into a **kernel that can hold a real European renovation project end-to-end**. The keystone is **KRN-V3-01 Phasing** — without it B1 is unbuildable. The "looks finished" gate is **KRN-V3-03 Multi-roof + fascia/gutter + soffit** — without all three the most photographed angle of any pitched-roof house reads naked. The unsung hero is **KRN-V3-04 Design Options** — the agent in T9 cannot work non-destructively without it, so T9's marquee bet (B2 Sketch-to-Deck) silently depends on shipping T1 first. **KRN-V3-02 Stacked walls** + **KRN-V3-05 Stair-by-sketch** + **KRN-V3-06 Plan region** round out the daily-architect workflows. Together the six `now`-state WPs are the B1 critical path; the six `next`-state WPs are the Wave-2 follow-on that lets a young architect feel that bim-ai has _no_ embarrassing kernel gaps. When T1's `now` cohort lands, B1's felt outcome — demolition phase grey-dashed, new phase bold-black, walls stacking timber-on-brick honestly, the L-shaped roof reading watertight from any 3D angle, design options A vs B kitchen toggle in one dropdown — is unblocked end-to-end. That is the moment a young architect can pick bim-ai over Revit for a residential renovation project, which is the moment B1 ships.
1208:- **T9 (AI-Native Tool Foundations).** `Cmd/Ctrl+K` palette is the entry point for invoking an external agent (Claude Code) for refinement (`"Ctrl+K → 'add a powder room near entry'"`). Helper dims + associative dims ground the agent's edits — bound dimensions update consistently when the external agent moves geometry via CMD-V3-01.
1233:**Why it matters.** T3 is the wedge for **B3 Live Collaboration with Time Machine** — the Figma-meets-Bluebeam bet. Revit's collaboration vocabulary (worksets, central files, "synchronize with central") is Subversion-era and reads as A6 in our anti-pattern catalog (R-D §5). Motif's deepest pillar today is collaboration semantics, not authoring (R-B §6) — Motif is "unambiguously deeper" than Rayon there, and that is the area where v3-T3 should learn most from Motif. Closing T3 unlocks the whole B3 felt-outcome — two architects co-author the model live, a third reviewer leaves twelve 3D-anchored comments on a sheet, and the activity stream is a real time machine. T3 is also the surface that pays back **D6 Comment & markup is first-class** and **D7 Activity stream is the time machine** — the two pillars that distinguish v3 from every Revit-clone in the market. 1300:**Acceptance.** Two browsers open the same model. User A drags a wall in 3D; User B's 3D view shows the same wall moving in 60 fps with a 2px halo at User A's color; the dim labels update on both sides. User A commits; the activity stream gains one row "User A — Move wall." Drop User A's WS connection mid-drag; on reconnect (CQ-01 robust path), state converges (yjs merges); no silent divergence. 3107:The tokenisation is a pure function from kernel state → token sequence and back. No AI runs inside `tkn/`. An external agent reads token sequences, edits them, submits through CMD-V3-01 — but the agent could equally be a CI script, a CSP solver, or a human typing JSON. The kernel reconstructs geometry deterministically; invalid tuples (host_id pointing at a deleted element, t out of [0..1]) are rejected with `tkn_invalid_token` advisories.
3136:#### CMD-V3-01 — Command-bundle apply API
3181:**Scope.** Every CMD-V3-01 bundle MUST carry a non-empty `assumptions: AssumptionEntry[]`. The kernel does **not** interpret semantics — it does not know whether `confidence: 0.7` is calibrated, does not validate that `source: "brief-line-12"` actually points at the brief. The kernel **only** validates _structure_: required fields, correct types, non-empty array, unique keys.
3197:**UI.** Inspector for any element with `agentTrace` shows an "Assumption log" section: list of entries; for `contestable: true` entries, an "Edit + re-run" button that opens a small form (key + value), submits a new bundle with the corrected value via CMD-V3-01.
3200:**Cross-theme references.** **API-V3-01.** **CMD-V3-01.** **T3 (activity stream renders assumption entries).** **§L (operational instrument of the locked decision).**
3204:_Source: T1 KRN-V3-04 (Design Options primitive). v3 §6 T9 anchor — the unsung hero of T9. T9's contribution to OPT-V3-01 is the **kernel-level non-destruction invariant** exposed as an enforced contract on CMD-V3-01: a bundle cannot directly mutate `main`._
3207:**Scope.** T1 ships the Design Options primitive. T9's contribution is the kernel-level invariant: **CMD-V3-01 cannot directly commit to `main`** unless the caller explicitly sets `targetOptionId: 'main'` AND has the `commit-to-main` capability (a UI-only capability for the manual "Promote to main" action). Every bundle without explicit `targetOptionId` lands as a new `DesignOption` inside an existing or fresh `DesignOptionSet`. This is the non-destruction property that makes 12-step refinement safe (TST-V3-01). Symmetric: humans hit "Promote to main" via deliberate click; agents cannot bypass that ceremony. Provenance recorded in the option's `provenance` field; activity stream renders option-creation events distinctly.

"""EDT-02 — geometric constraint evaluator (load-bearing slice).

A constraint is a small Pydantic record (``ConstraintElem``) whose
``rule`` ties two element groups together. After every command apply,
the engine asks this module to evaluate every constraint against the
new world. If any ``error``-severity constraint reports a residual
greater than ``EPSILON_MM``, the command is rejected and the world
rolls back.

The padlock UI authors a single constraint per click: clicking the
padlock on a temporary dimension between two walls captures the
current distance as ``locked_value_mm`` on a fresh ``equal_distance``
constraint between those walls.

The slice supports the most common rule (``equal_distance`` between
two walls' centres) — enough to make the engine behaviour testable
end-to-end. Other rules (parallel / perpendicular / collinear /
equal_length) are stubbed with pass-through evaluators that return
``ok`` so the engine never blocks on a rule it does not yet fully
implement; future work can fill those in without changing the
calling protocol.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass
from math import hypot
from typing import Any

EPSILON_MM = 0.5

input.content:
# WP-002 — EDT-V3-01: Geometric Constraint Rules

**Branch:** `feat/v3-edt-v3-01-constraint-rules`
**Effort:** L — 2.5 weeks
**State:** `now` (re-dispatch after failed first attempt)

## Required reading

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T2 EDT-V3-01 entry
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/edt/constraints.py` — 184-line file with `equal_distance` implemented; parallel/perpendicular/collinear/equal_length are **stubs that always return ok**
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/commands.py` — `CreateConstraintCmd` and `ConstraintRule` enum
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/elements.py` — `ConstraintElem`, `ConstraintRefRow`, `WallElem`, `GridLineElem`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine.py` — how `evaluate_constraints` is called after `apply_inplace`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/plan/GripLayer.tsx` — grip halo rendering (constraint lock icon)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/tests/` — look at existing constraint tests for pattern

## WP-002 verbatim from tracker (§6 T2 EDT-V3-01)

**EDT-V3-01 — Constraint rules: parallel / perpendicular / collinear / equal_length**

**Status.** `now`.

**Scope.** Four geometric constraint evaluators that the engine calls after every command apply. Each evaluator receives the two `refsA` / `refsB` element groups and the current element dictionary; it returns a `residual_mm` (or `residual_deg` for angular rules) that the engine compares against `EPSILON_MM`. If any `error`-severity constraint fires, the command is rejected and the world rolls back.

- **parallel** — the two wall/grid-line direction vectors are parallel (cross-product magnitude ≤ EPSILON_MM per unit length). `residual` = cross-product magnitude.
- **perpendicular** — dot product of direction vectors ≤ EPSILON_MM. `residual` = |dot product|.
- **collinear** — two walls lie on the same infinite line (direction parallel AND offset between start points projected onto the normal ≤ EPSILON_MM). `residual` = max(angle residual, offset residual).
- **equal_length** — the two wall/beam lengths are equal (|len_a − len_b| ≤ EPSILON_MM). `residual` = |len_a − len_b|.

**Data model.** `ConstraintRule` literal already has `parallel | perpendicular | collinear | equal_length` entries in `commands.py`. The `ConstraintRefRow` already carries `elementId` + `edge: Literal["start", "end", "axis"] | None`. No schema changes needed.

**Engine.** Replace the four stub evaluators in `app/bim_ai/edt/constraints.py` with real implementations. Each must:
1. Extract direction vectors from `WallElem` or `GridLineElem` via `(end - start) / |end - start|`.
2. Compute the geometric residual.
3. Return a `ConstraintResult(residual_mm=..., ok=residual <= EPSILON_MM)`.

**UI.** `GripLayer.tsx` already renders a padlock icon for `equal_distance` constraints. Add padlock overlays for the four new rules using the same `--constraint-lock-colour` CSS token. No new icon needed — same padlock glyph, different rule label in the tooltip.

**Acceptance.**
- `pytest app/tests/test_constraint_rules.py` — all tests pass (write them: ≥ 4 × 3 = 12 tests covering ok + violation + edge cases for each rule).
- Engine rejects a `moveWallDelta` that would violate a `perpendicular` constraint with severity `error`.
- Engine allows the same move when constraint severity is `warning`.
- `parallel` and `collinear` evaluators handle zero-length input without crashing.

**Effort.** L — 2.5 weeks.

**Cross-theme references.** T2 (grip provider uses constraint state to show lock icon); T9 (CMD-V3-01 bundles that move geometry trigger constraint re-evaluation post-apply).

## Setup

```bash
git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only && git checkout -b feat/v3-edt-v3-01-constraint-rules
```

## Implementation tasks

1. **Read `constraints.py` end-to-end** — understand `ConstraintResult`, `evaluate_constraints`, and the existing `equal_distance` implementation as the reference pattern.

2. **Implement `parallel`** — extract direction vectors from both ref elements; compute cross-product magnitude; return `ConstraintResult(residual_mm=cross_mag, ok=cross_mag <= EPSILON_MM)`.

3. **Implement `perpendicular`** — compute |dot product| of normalised direction vectors; residual = |dot|.

4. **Implement `collinear`** — parallel check PLUS: project the vector between the two start points onto the shared normal; residual = max(angle_residual, offset_mm).

5. **Implement `equal_length`** — compute lengths via `hypot`; residual = |len_a - len_b|.

6. **Write `app/tests/test_constraint_rules.py`** — ≥ 12 tests: for each rule, test (a) ok case, (b) violation rejected, (c) edge case (zero-length, parallel walls with offset). Follow the style of existing engine tests.

7. **GripLayer.tsx** — add padlock overlay rendering for the four new rules. Look at how `equal_distance` padlocks are rendered and replicate for the new rules. Use the same CSS token.

## Implementation rules

- Do NOT change `ConstraintRule` in `commands.py` — the four literals are already there.
- Do NOT modify `ConstraintElem` in `elements.py` — schema is frozen.
- The evaluators must be **pure functions** — no side effects, no I/O.
- Zero-length walls must not crash: guard with `if length < EPSILON_MM: return ConstraintResult(residual_mm=0.0, ok=True)` (a zero-length wall satisfies any orientation constraint vacuously).
- Per API-V3-01 contract: if you add any new CLI surface, register it in the tool registry. For this WP, no new commands are needed — constraint evaluation is internal.

## Verify gate

```bash
cd app && python -m pytest tests/test_constraint_rules.py -v
make verify
cd .. && pnpm tsc --noEmit
```

## Commit and push

```bash
git add -p
git commit -m "feat(edt): EDT-V3-01 constraint rules — parallel/perpendicular/collinear/equal_length"
git push -u origin feat/v3-edt-v3-01-constraint-rules
```

## Final report

Paste this back to the orchestrator when done:

```
WP: WP-002 / EDT-V3-01
Branch: feat/v3-edt-v3-01-constraint-rules
Status: done
Tests: <N> passed
Files changed: <list key files>
Notes: <anything unexpected>
```

aiTitle: Bootstrap v3 Orchestrator and dispatch Sprint 0 WPs

attachment.snippet:
1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from typing import Annotated, Any, Literal
4	
5	from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, model_validator
6	
7	from bim_ai.elements import (
8	    CameraMm,
9	    ConstraintRefRow,
10	    ConstraintRule,
11	    CurtainPanelOverride,
12	    DormerPositionOnRoof,
13	    DormerRoofKind,
14	    DxfLineworkPrim,
15	    EvidenceRef,
16	    PlanCategoryGraphicRow,
17	    PlanTagBadgeStyle,
18	    PlanTagTarget,
19	    RoomColorSchemeRow,
20	    SiteContextObjectRow,
21	    StairLanding,
22	    StairRun,
23	    SweepPathPoint,
24	    SweepProfilePlane,
25	    SweepProfilePoint,
26	    Text3dFontFamily,
27	    Vec2Mm,
28	    Vec3Mm,
29	    WallRecessZone,
30	    WallTypeLayer,
31	)
32	from bim_ai.roof_geometry import RoofGeometryMode
33	
34	
35	class CreateLevelCmd(BaseModel):
36	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
37	    type: Literal["createLevel"] = "createLevel"
38	    id: str | None = None
39	    name: str = "Level"
40	    elevation_mm: float = Field(alias="elevationMm", default=0)
41	    datum_kind: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="datumKind")
42	    parent_level_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="parentLevelId")
43	    offset_from_parent_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="offsetFromParentMm")
44	    # VIE-05: when True (default), the engine also creates a "<name> — Plan"
45	    # plan_view referencing the new level so the common flow needs no follow-up.
46	    also_create_plan_view: bool = Field(default=True, alias="alsoCreatePlanView")
47	    plan_view_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="planViewId")
48	
49	
50	class CreateWallCmd(BaseModel):
51	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
52	    type: Literal["createWall"] = "createWall"
53	    id: str | None = None
54	    name: str = "Wall"
55	    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
56	    start: Vec2Mm
57	    end: Vec2Mm
58	    thickness_mm: float = Field(alias="thicknessMm", default=200)
59	    height_mm: float = Field(alias="heightMm", default=2800)
60	    wall_type_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="wallTypeId")
61	    base_constraint_level_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="baseConstraintLevelId")
62	    top_constraint_level_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="topConstraintLevelId")
63	    base_constraint_offset_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="baseConstraintOffsetMm")
64	    top_constraint_offset_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="topConstraintOffsetMm")
65	    insulation_extension_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="insulationExtensionMm")
66	    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")
67	    is_curtain_wall: bool = Field(default=False, alias="isCurtainWall")
68	
69	
70	class MoveWallDeltaCmd(BaseModel):
71	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
72	    type: Literal["moveWallDelta"] = "moveWallDelta"
73	    wall_id: str = Field(alias="wallId")
74	    dx_mm: float = Field(alias="dxMm")
75	    dy_mm: float = Field(alias="dyMm")
76	    force_pin_override: bool = Field(default=False, alias="forcePinOverride")
77	
78	
79	class MoveWallEndpointsCmd(BaseModel):
80	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
81	    type: Literal["moveWallEndpoints"] = "moveWallEndpoints"
82	    wall_id: str = Field(alias="wallId")
83	    start: Vec2Mm
84	    end: Vec2Mm
85	    force_pin_override: bool = Field(default=False, alias="forcePinOverride")
86	
87	
88	class MoveBeamEndpointsCmd(BaseModel):
89	    """EDT-01 propagation — beam endpoints move command.
90	
91	    Beams are not yet seeded into the Python store (see `elements.py` —
92	    no `BeamElem` defined), so the engine handler today rejects with a
93	    clear "not implemented" message. The command schema lives here so
94	    the TS grip provider can emit a stable shape that the engine slice
95	    can adopt without a TS rebuild.
96	    """

Everything is running. Here's where things stand:

Background agents in flight:
- WP-002 implementation: writing the real EDT constraint evaluators (parallel/perpendicular/collinear/equal_length) in a clean worktree
- WP-009 prompt: CMD-V3-01 command-bundle apply
- WP-010 prompt: KRN-V3-02 stacked walls
- WP-011 prompt: KRN-V3-03 multi-roof + fascia/gutter + soffit
- WP-012 prompt: KRN-V3-05 stair-by-sketch
- WP-013 prompt: CQ-01 WebSocket robustness
- WP-014 + WP-015 prompts: CHR-V3-01 top-bar + CHR-V3-02 workspace switcher

Once they complete, I'll merge WP-002 and commit all the prompt files. At that point you'll have 7 prompts ready — run each as `please implement spec/v3-prompts/wp-009.md` through `wp-015.md` in separate sessions. I'll notify you when everything is ready.

<result>The file has been written. Here is a summary of what was produced:

**`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/v3-prompts/wp-010.md`** — 215 lines, fully self-contained.

Key decisions made while authoring:

1. **Data model** — `stack` is added as an optional field on the existing `WallElem` rather than a new element kind. `WallStackComponent` + `WallStack` are new Pydantic models in `elements.py`, mirrored as TypeScript types in `packages/core/src/index.ts`. Neither `ElementKind` nor `ElemKind` changes.

2. **New command** — `SetWallStackCmd` (type `"setWallStack"`) replaces the stack on an existing wall. `CreateWallCmd` gets an optional `stackComponents` field for atomic creation. Both are wired into the `Command` union.

3. **Validation** — `_validate_wall_stack` enforces that prefix Σ heightMm < wall.height_mm (last component is remainder, not bounded above).

4. **Pure helpers** — `resolve_stack_wall_type_at_cut` (for plan renderers to pick the right hatch at a given cut-plane height) and `schedule_stacked_components` (for schedule queries) are specified as pure functions with exact signatures.

5. **WP-004 overlap warning** — explicit rebase instruction: branch from merged `main`, do not touch phasing fields, conflict-resolution hint for the `match cmd:` dispatch block.

6. **API-V3-01 / no-hex rules** — implementation rules section covers both.

7. **Ten required pytest cases** — covering the acceptance demo scenario (6 m wall, brick 3 m + cladding 3 m, cut at 1 000 mm vs 4 000 mm) plus edge cases.</result>

<result>Done. The file is at `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/v3-prompts/wp-013.md`.

Here is what was written and why each choice was made:

**Structure (10 sections, ~220 lines):**

1. **Header** — branch `feat/v3-cq-01-ws-robustness`, effort M / 1.5 weeks, state `now`.

2. **Required reading** — absolute paths to all six files read during research, including the exact line number for `websocket_loop` in `routes_api.py` (1178) so the agent finds it immediately.

3. **Verbatim CQ-01 entry** — copied word-for-word from `spec/code-quality-tracker.md` so the agent has the acceptance criteria in front of it without having to cross-reference.

4. **Setup bash block** — `git checkout -b` + `make verify` green-gate before touching anything.

5. **Five implementation tasks** grounded in what the code actually looks like:
   - Task 1 targets the exact 88-LOC `hub.py` with the fire-and-forget `broadcast_json` pattern.
   - Task 2 adds `resumeFrom: int | None = Query(default=None)` to the existing `websocket_loop`, gating the current unconditional snapshot send.
   - Task 3 replaces the raw `new WebSocket(...)` in `useWorkspaceSnapshot.ts` (the file with `ws.onclose = () => setWsOn(false)` and no reconnect) with a `connectWs` helper plus backoff schedule (250 ms → 8 s, jittered, 10 attempts max) and `lastSeqRef`.
   - Task 4 migrates delta broadcast callsites to `hub.publish` while leaving presence frames on `broadcast_json`.
   - Task 5 specifies three concrete pytest-asyncio tests matching the acceptance criteria exactly.

6. **Implementation rules** — no seq on presence frames, in-process ring buffer only, TypeScript strict, visibility-change guard on backoff, keep diff minimal.

7. **Prerequisite note** — names COL-V3-01, OUT-V3-01, and JOB-V3-01 as hard-blocked, citing the tracker sections that say so.

8. **Verify gate** — `make verify` must print `PASS`.

9. **Commit and push** — explicit `git add` of the four changed/new files.

10. **Final-report template** — structured completion report the agent fills in.</result>

content.content:
commit 8802d8ab4cb79336fbe94311ed5d5b5c2ec2908b
Author: Johannes Hötter <jhoetter@MacBook-Pro-von-Johannes.local>
Date:   Fri May 8 17:21:33 2026 +0200

    feat(edt): EDT-V3-01 constraint rules — parallel/perpendicular/collinear/equal_length
    
    Implements the four geometric constraint evaluators in
    app/bim_ai/edt/constraints.py that were previously pass-through stubs:
    
    - parallel: cross-product magnitude of normalised direction vectors
    - perpendicular: |dot product| of normalised direction vectors
    - collinear: parallel check + normal-projection offset between start points
    - equal_length: |len_a - len_b| via hypot
    
    All evaluators guard zero-length inputs (vacuous pass), handle both
    wall and grid_line kinds for orientation rules, and follow the
    existing ConstraintViolation / evaluate_constraint protocol so the
    engine's post-apply rejection path works unchanged.
    
    GripLayer.tsx gains PADLOCK_RULES / RULE_LABEL constants and the
    constraintRule prop on TempDimLayerProps so the padlock chip shows
    rule-specific tooltips for all five constraint rules.
    
    26 tests added in app/tests/test_constraint_rules.py covering ok /
    violation / edge-case paths for each rule plus engine-level rejection
    and warning pass-through integration tests.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    Entire-Checkpoint: 1da09f9064a5

 app/bim_ai/edt/constraints.py       | 187 +++++++++++++-
 app/tests/test_constraint_rules.py  | 468 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 packages/web/src/plan/GripLayer.tsx |  35 ++-
 3 files changed, 681 insertions(+), 9 deletions(-)

commit c1f5f3b41152755a481e1388c729b06496d54d47
Author: Johannes Hötter <jhoetter@MacBook-Pro-von-Johannes.local>
Date:   Fri May 8 17:36:45 2026 +0200

    feat(ws): CQ-01 WebSocket robustness — seq + ring buffer + backoff reconnect
    
    - Hub: per-model monotonic seq counter, deque(maxlen=512) ring buffer,
      publish()/resume() API, per-socket backpressure (threshold=8, close 1011)
    - Route: resumeFrom query param; replay window hit sends deltas + replay_done,
      miss sends RESYNC; initial connect still sends full snapshot
    - Client: connectWs() helper, lastSeqRef, exponential backoff reconnect
      (250ms→8s, ±20% jitter, 10 attempts, visibilitychange deferral,
      mountedRef guard), RESYNC handler refetches snapshot
    - Migrated two delta broadcast_json calls to hub.publish() for seq stamping
    - Tests: 3 new integration tests (replay-hit, replay-miss, backpressure)
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

    AssumptionEntry structural validation, apply_bundle() pure function.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

The user is running the bim-ai v3 build programme with Claude as a CTO-level orchestrator. The session resumed from a previous context-limited conversation where 8 work-package (WP) prompt files had been written. The explicit requests in this session were:
- Validate and merge all 8 wave-1 WPs (WP-001 through WP-008) into main
- Confirm whether sprint 0 (WP-001) must complete before others start (answer: only before CHR-V3-* WPs)
- Provide 7 copy-paste prompts for the user to run in separate sessions
- After all workers claimed done: check, validate, and merge them all
- "do the work needed until i can spawn the next agents" — autonomously implement WP-002 (EDT constraints that had no commits), write prompt files for the next wave (WP-009 through WP-015), commit state to git
- After wave-2 workers claimed done: check, validate, merge again

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- bim-ai v3 programme: ~115 work packages across 9 strategic themes + chrome + CQ track
- Sprint 0 / B4 phase 1: tokens-v3.css, brand-layer.css, ESLint no-hex-in-chrome rule, brand-swap CI — must merge before any CHR-V3-* WPs
- Cherry-pick strategy: workers contaminate branches with unrelated seed/viewport commits; orchestrator cherry-picks only the specific WP commit SHA
- Wave parallelism: up to 8 workers simultaneously; workers started from slightly different base commits causing conflicts in shared files
- Conflict resolution pattern: all conflicts are additive (multiple WPs appending to commands.py Command union, engine.py dispatch block, cli.mjs subcommand handlers) — always include ALL new content
- Key shared files prone to conflicts: `app/bim_ai/commands.py` (Command union), `app/bim_ai/engine.py` (apply_inplace match block), `app/bim_ai/routes_api.py` (API endpoints), `packages/cli/cli.mjs` (subcommand handlers), `packages/core/src/index.ts` (TypeScript Element union)
- API-V3-01 contract: every new kernel verb must add CLI + REST/RPC + JSON schema + assumption-log
- TKN-V3-01 (tokenised representation): gating keystone for CMD-V3-01, OPT-V3-01, IMG-V3-01
- CMD-V3-01: command-bundle apply (dry-run/commit/tolerate modes, parent_revision optimistic lock, AssumptionEntry validation)
- CQ-01 (WebSocket robustness): hard prerequisite for COL-V3-01 (multi-user), OUT-V3-01, JOB-V3-01
- Pre-existing coverage gate failure (23% vs 65% required) is NOT a new regression — pre-existing project-wide issue

3. Files and Code Sections:

- `spec/v3-build-state.md` — Orchestrator state tracker. Created fresh (was never committed in previous session). Contains live workers table, merged WPs table, held-back list, file-overlap notes. Final state: wave-1 all 8 merged, wave-2 4/7 merged, WP-010 and WP-012 failed.

- `spec/v3-prompts/wp-001.md` through `wp-008.md` — Implementation prompts written by background agents in previous session. Files were lost (worktree cleanup) but original implementations already merged to main. `wp-002.md` rewritten manually in this session.

- `spec/v3-prompts/wp-002.md` — EDT-V3-01 constraint rules prompt. Written directly by orchestrator. Specifies: implement parallel/perpendicular/collinear/equal_length evaluators in `app/bim_ai/edt/constraints.py` (replacing pass-through stubs), write ≥12 tests in `test_constraint_rules.py`, add padlock overlays in `GripLayer.tsx`.

- `spec/v3-prompts/wp-009.md` through `wp-015.md` — Next-wave prompts written by background prompt-writer agents: CMD-V3-01, KRN-V3-02, KRN-V3-03, KRN-V3-05, CQ-01, CHR-V3-01, CHR-V3-02.

- `app/bim_ai/commands.py` — Major conflict resolution file. Wave-1 merge added: PhaseFilter import, CreateSunSettingsCmd, UpdateSunSettingsCmd, CreatePhaseCmd, RenamePhaseCmd, ReorderPhaseCmd, DeletePhaseCmd, SetElementPhaseCmd, SetViewPhaseCmd, SetViewPhaseFilterCmd, MoveElementCmd to both class definitions and the `Command = Annotated[...]` union. Wave-2 KRN-V3-03 added RoofJoinElem, EdgeProfileRunElem, SoffitElem commands (60 lines).

- `app/bim_ai/engine.py` — Conflict resolution for multiple WPs appending to the `match cmd:` block. After resolution contains: phasing dispatch cases, MoveElementCmd case, plan region cases, sun settings cases (from WP-004 cherry-pick which bundled sun settings commands). Wave-2 added KRN-V3-03 dispatch cases (160 lines).

- `app/bim_ai/routes_api.py` — Conflict: HEAD had API-V3-01 tool registry routes (from WP-003), WP-005 wanted TKN-V3-01 token encode/decode/diff endpoints. Resolution: kept both sections separated by headers. Wave-2 CQ-01 added WebSocket resumeFrom/RESYNC logic (148 insertions).

- `packages/cli/cli.mjs` — Conflict: HEAD had `tokens` subcommand (WP-005), WP-006 had `plan-region` subcommand. Resolution: kept both blocks with `process.exit(1)` at end of tokens block before plan-region block starts. Wave-2 CMD-V3-01 added apply-bundle --base/--dry-run/--commit/--tolerate/--assumptions flags (106 insertions).

- `app/bim_ai/hub.py` — WP-013 (CQ-01) added per-model monotonic seq counter, deque(512) ring buffer, publish()/resume() API, per-socket backpressure (50 insertions).

- `packages/web/src/workspace/useWorkspaceSnapshot.ts` — WP-013 (CQ-01) added connectWs() helper with exponential backoff (250ms→8s, ±20% jitter, 10 attempts, visibilitychange guard), lastSeqRef, RESYNC handler (144 insertions).

- `app/bim_ai/cmd/` — New module from WP-009 (CMD-V3-01): `types.py` (CommandBundle, BundleResult, AssumptionEntry), `apply_bundle.py` (pure function with dry-run/commit/tolerate modes, parent_revision optimistic lock).

- `app/bim_ai/edt/constraints.py` — WP-002 replaced 4 pass-through stubs with real geometric evaluators (187 insertions + 9 deletions). Three private helpers: `_direction_vector`, `_element_length`, `_resolve_first_element`, `_violation`.

- `app/bim_ai/site/sun_position.py` — WP-007 (SUN-V3-01) NOAA solar position algorithm, pure Python deterministic, Munich 21 Jun 14:30 CEST reference case (197 lines).

- `packages/web/src/viewport/SunOverlay.tsx` — WP-007 collapsible sun settings panel, CSS variables only (265 lines).

- `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx` — WP-014+015 bundled: 8-slot v3 top bar, all colors CSS variables, --radius-canvas:0, --ease-paper transitions (334 lines).

- `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx` — WP-015: disc-* left-border stripe, 4-row listbox, 240ms ease-paper flip, no hex (202 lines).

- `app/bim_ai/tkn/` — WP-005 new module: `encode.py`, `decode.py`, `diff.py`, `validate.py`, `types.py` — pure-function token layer (TokenSequence, EntityToken, EnvelopeToken).

4. Errors and Fixes:

- **WP-002 and WP-007 had 0 commits**: WP-002 (EDT) worker opened branch but committed nothing. WP-007 (SUN) commit landed on feat/v3-krn-v3-01-phasing (phasing branch). Fix: cherry-picked WP-007 from the phasing branch SHA `ab34882d`. WP-002 re-dispatched as a worktree implementation agent.

- **WP-004 cherry-pick bundled SUN commands**: Commit `31cae0bb` (phasing) added BOTH phasing commands AND sun settings commands (CreateSunSettingsCmd, UpdateSunSettingsCmd) to commands.py. This was by design — the sun worker built on the phasing branch. Resolved by accepting HEAD content (keeping all commands) when WP-006's diff tried to delete the SUN section.

- **WP-005 conflicts with WP-004** in commands.py, engine.py, routes_api.py: All additive conflicts. Resolution: include both WP-004's phasing additions AND WP-005's MoveElementCmd / token endpoints.

- **WP-006 cli.mjs conflict**: HEAD had full tokens subcommand block (from WP-005), WP-006 had plan-region subcommand. The tokens block was missing its closing `process.exit(1)`. Fix: added `process.exit(1)` at end of tokens block, then plan-region block follows.

- **Stray `<<<<<<< HEAD` marker in engine.py**: After editing WP-005/WP-004 conflict, the `<<<<<<< HEAD` line at line 3794 was not removed in the first pass. Found via second `grep` pass, removed separately.

- **State file not committed**: `spec/v3-build-state.md` was created in the previous session but never committed to git. Also `spec/v3-prompts/` files were lost when worktrees were cleaned up. Fix: recreated both from scratch.

- **Wrong branch during cherry-pick** (wave-2): Was on `feat/v3-krn-v3-03-multi-roof` when cherry-picking CQ-01. Committed to wrong branch. Fix: `git reset HEAD~1`, stash, `git checkout main`, remove stray test file, re-cherry-pick.

- **Wave-2 branch contamination**: Workers committed to wrong branches. KRN-V3-03 and CMD-V3-01 commits ended up stacked on feat/v3-cq-01-ws-robustness locally. CQ-01 had a duplicate on feat/v3-krn-v3-03-multi-roof. Fix: used `git log --all --oneline` to find commits by message regardless of branch, then cherry-picked by SHA.

- **Untracked file blocking cherry-pick**: `app/tests/test_ws_robustness.py` was left as untracked after reset. Fix: `rm app/tests/test_ws_robustness.py` before re-cherry-picking.

- **Markdown table rendering bug**: User screenshot showed strikethrough artifacts on hyphenated text (WP-002, KRN-V3-*) in Claude Code terminal due to font ligatures. Fix: saved feedback memory, switched to plain text lists only.

5. Problem Solving:

- **Cherry-pick vs branch merge**: Chose cherry-pick for all WPs because worker branches contained unrelated seed/viewport commits from parallel sessions. Cherry-picking the specific WP commit SHA kept main clean.
- **Additive conflict resolution**: All conflicts in commands.py, engine.py, routes_api.py, cli.mjs were additive (multiple workers appending to same files). Resolution pattern: always include ALL new content from both sides.
- **WP-007 on wrong branch**: Cherry-picked `ab34882d` directly from the phasing branch rather than requiring re-implementation.
- **Missing WP-010, WP-012**: Workers never implemented these. State file updated to show `failed` status. Prompts `wp-010.md` and `wp-012.md` remain committed for re-dispatch.

# WP-002 — EDT-V3-01: Geometric Constraint Rules

**Branch:** `feat/v3-edt-v3-01-constraint-rules`
**Effort:** L — 2.5 weeks
**State:** `now` (re-dispatch after failed first attempt)

## Required reading

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T2 EDT-V3-01 entry
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/edt/constraints.py` — 184-line file with `equal_distance` implemented; parallel/perpendicular/collinear/equal_length are **stubs that always return ok**
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/commands.py` — `CreateConstraintCmd` and `ConstraintRule` enum
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/elements.py` — `ConstraintElem`, `ConstraintRefRow`, `WallElem`, `GridLineElem`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine.py` — how `evaluate_constraints` is called after `apply_inplace`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/plan/GripLayer.tsx` — grip halo rendering (constraint lock icon)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/tests/` — look at existing constraint tests for pattern

## WP-002 verbatim from tracker (§6 T2 EDT-V3-01)

**EDT-V3-01 — Constraint rules: parallel / perpendicular / collinear / equal_length**

**Status.** `now`.

**Scope.** Four geometric constraint evaluators that the engine calls after every command apply. Each evaluator receives the two `refsA` / `refsB` element groups and the current element dictionary; it returns a `residual_mm` (or `residual_deg` for angular rules) that the engine compares against `EPSILON_MM`. If any `error`-severity constraint fires, the command is rejected and the world rolls back.

- **parallel** — the two wall/grid-line direction vectors are parallel (cross-product magnitude ≤ EPSILON_MM per unit length). `residual` = cross-product magnitude.
- **perpendicular** — dot product of direction vectors ≤ EPSILON_MM. `residual` = |dot product|.
- **collinear** — two walls lie on the same infinite line (direction parallel AND offset between start points projected onto the normal ≤ EPSILON_MM). `residual` = max(angle residual, offset residual).
- **equal_length** — the two wall/beam lengths are equal (|len_a − len_b| ≤ EPSILON_MM). `residual` = |len_a − len_b|.

**Data model.** `ConstraintRule` literal already has `parallel | perpendicular | collinear | equal_length` entries in `commands.py`. The `ConstraintRefRow` already carries `elementId` + `edge: Literal["start", "end", "axis"] | None`. No schema changes needed.

**Engine.** Replace the four stub evaluators in `app/bim_ai/edt/constraints.py` with real implementations. Each must:
1. Extract direction vectors from `WallElem` or `GridLineElem` via `(end - start) / |end - start|`.
2. Compute the geometric residual.
3. Return a `ConstraintResult(residual_mm=..., ok=residual <= EPSILON_MM)`.

**UI.** `GripLayer.tsx` already renders a padlock icon for `equal_distance` constraints. Add padlock overlays for the four new rules using the same `--constraint-lock-colour` CSS token. No new icon needed — same padlock glyph, different rule label in the tooltip.

**Acceptance.**
- `pytest app/tests/test_constraint_rules.py` — all tests pass (write them: ≥ 4 × 3 = 12 tests covering ok + violation + edge cases for each rule).
- Engine rejects a `moveWallDelta` that would violate a `perpendicular` constraint with severity `error`.
- Engine allows the same move when constraint severity is `warning`.
- `parallel` and `collinear` evaluators handle zero-length input without crashing.

**Effort.** L — 2.5 weeks.

**Cross-theme references.** T2 (grip provider uses constraint state to show lock icon); T9 (CMD-V3-01 bundles that move geometry trigger constraint re-evaluation post-apply).

## Setup

```bash
git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only && git checkout -b feat/v3-edt-v3-01-constraint-rules
```

## Implementation tasks

1. **Read `constraints.py` end-to-end** — understand `ConstraintResult`, `evaluate_constraints`, and the existing `equal_distance` implementation as the reference pattern.

2. **Implement `parallel`** — extract direction vectors from both ref elements; compute cross-product magnitude; return `ConstraintResult(residual_mm=cross_mag, ok=cross_mag <= EPSILON_MM)`.

3. **Implement `perpendicular`** — compute |dot product| of normalised direction vectors; residual = |dot|.

4. **Implement `collinear`** — parallel check PLUS: project the vector between the two start points onto the shared normal; residual = max(angle_residual, offset_mm).

5. **Implement `equal_length`** — compute lengths via `hypot`; residual = |len_a - len_b|.

6. **Write `app/tests/test_constraint_rules.py`** — ≥ 12 tests: for each rule, test (a) ok case, (b) violation rejected, (c) edge case (zero-length, parallel walls with offset). Follow the style of existing engine tests.

7. **GripLayer.tsx** — add padlock overlay rendering for the four new rules. Look at how `equal_distance` padlocks are rendered and replicate for the new rules. Use the same CSS token.

## Implementation rules

- Do NOT change `ConstraintRule` in `commands.py` — the four literals are already there.
- Do NOT modify `ConstraintElem` in `elements.py` — schema is frozen.
- The evaluators must be **pure functions** — no side effects, no I/O.
- Zero-length walls must not crash: guard with `if length < EPSILON_MM: return ConstraintResult(residual_mm=0.0, ok=True)` (a zero-length wall satisfies any orientation constraint vacuously).
- Per API-V3-01 contract: if you add any new CLI surface, register it in the tool registry. For this WP, no new commands are needed — constraint evaluation is internal.

## Verify gate

```bash
cd app && python -m pytest tests/test_constraint_rules.py -v
make verify
cd .. && pnpm tsc --noEmit
```

## Commit and push

```bash
git add -p
git commit -m "feat(edt): EDT-V3-01 constraint rules — parallel/perpendicular/collinear/equal_length"
git push -u origin feat/v3-edt-v3-01-constraint-rules
```

## Final report

Paste this back to the orchestrator when done:

```
WP: WP-002 / EDT-V3-01
Branch: feat/v3-edt-v3-01-constraint-rules
Status: done
Tests: <N> passed
Files changed: <list key files>
Notes: <anything unexpected>
```

21	BRICK_TYPE = "wt-brick"

42	        "levelId": LEVEL_ID,
43	        "start": {"xMm": 0, "yMm": 0},

45	        "heightMm": height_mm,
46	    }
47	    if stack_components is not None:
48	        cmd["stackComponents"] = stack_components
49	    return cmd
50	
51	
52	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	# CreateWallCmd with stackComponents
54	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	
56	
57	def test_create_wall_with_stack_atomically():
58	    doc = _base_doc()
59	    ok, new_doc, _, _, code = try_commit_bundle(
60	        doc,
