case CreateWallCmd():
            eid = cmd.id or new_id()
            if eid in els:
                raise ValueError(f"duplicate element id '{eid}'")
            if cmd.level_id not in els or not isinstance(els[cmd.level_id], LevelElem):
                raise ValueError("createWall.levelId must reference an existing Level")
            h_mm = _resolve_wall_height_mm(cmd, els)
            thick = _wall_thickness_from_type(els, cmd.wall_type_id, cmd.thickness_mm)
            els[eid] = WallElem(
                kind="wall",
                id=eid,
                name=cmd.name,
                level_id=cmd.level_id,
                start=cmd.start,
                end=cmd.end,
                thickness_mm=thick,
                height_mm=h_mm,
                wall_type_id=cmd.wall_type_id,
                base_constraint_level_id=cmd.base_constraint_level_id,
                top_constraint_level_id=cmd.top_constraint_level_id,
                base_constraint_offset_mm=cmd.base_constraint_offset_mm,
                top_constraint_offset_mm=cmd.top_constraint_offset_mm,
                insulation_extension_mm=cmd.insulation_extension_mm,
                material_key=cmd.material_key,
                is_curtain_wall=cmd.is_curtain_wall,
            )

        case MoveWallDeltaCmd():
            w = els.get(cmd.wall_id)
            if not isinstance(w, WallElem):
                raise ValueError("move_wall_delta.wallId must reference a Wall")
            sx = w.start.model_copy(
                update={"x_mm": w.start.x_mm + cmd.dx_mm, "y_mm": w.start.y_mm + cmd.dy_mm}
            )
            sy = w.end.model_copy(
                update={"x_mm": w.end.x_mm + cmd.dx_mm, "y_mm": w.end.y_mm + cmd.dy_mm}
            )
            els[cmd.wall_id] = w.model_copy(update={"start": sx, "end": sy})

        case MoveWallEndpointsCmd():
            w = els.get(cmd.wall_id)
            if not isinstance(w, WallElem):
                raise ValueError("move_wall_endpoints.wallId must reference a Wall")
            els[cmd.wall_id] = w.model_copy(update={"start": cmd.start, "end": cmd.end})

        case MoveBeamEndpointsCmd():
            # EDT-01 propagation — beams aren't yet stored in the
            # Python engine (no `BeamElem` in elements.py). The grip
            # provider emits this command shape so the kernel slice
            # can land later without a TS rebuild. Reject explicitly so
            # bad replays surface, rather than silently no-op.
            raise ValueError(
                "moveBeamEndpoints: beam elements are not yet seeded in the engine; "
                "TS grips emit this command for forward compatibility (EDT-01 propagation)"
            )

        case SetCurtainPanelOverrideCmd():
            wall = els.get(cmd.wall_id)
            if not isinstance(wall, WallElem):
                raise ValueError("setCurtainPanelOverride.wallId must reference a Wall")

class CreateWallCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createWall"] = "createWall"
    id: str | None = None
    name: str = "Wall"
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    start: Vec2Mm
    end: Vec2Mm
    thickness_mm: float = Field(alias="thicknessMm", default=200)
    height_mm: float = Field(alias="heightMm", default=2800)
    wall_type_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="wallTypeId")
    base_constraint_level_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="baseConstraintLevelId")
    top_constraint_level_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="topConstraintLevelId")
    base_constraint_offset_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="baseConstraintOffsetMm")
    top_constraint_offset_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="topConstraintOffsetMm")
    insulation_extension_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="insulationExtensionMm")
    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")
    is_curtain_wall: bool = Field(default=False, alias="isCurtainWall")


class MoveWallDeltaCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["moveWallDelta"] = "moveWallDelta"
    wall_id: str = Field(alias="wallId")
    dx_mm: float = Field(alias="dxMm")
    dy_mm: float = Field(alias="dyMm")
    force_pin_override: bool = Field(default=False, alias="forcePinOverride")


class MoveWallEndpointsCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["moveWallEndpoints"] = "moveWallEndpoints"
    wall_id: str = Field(alias="wallId")
    start: Vec2Mm
    end: Vec2Mm
    force_pin_override: bool = Field(default=False, alias="forcePinOverride")


class MoveBeamEndpointsCmd(BaseModel):
    """EDT-01 propagation — beam endpoints move command.

    Beams are not yet seeded into the Python store (see `elements.py` —
    no `BeamElem` defined), so the engine handler today rejects with a
    clear "not implemented" message. The command schema lives here so
    the TS grip provider can emit a stable shape that the engine slice
    can adopt without a TS rebuild.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["moveBeamEndpoints"] = "moveBeamEndpoints"
    beam_id: str = Field(alias="beamId")
    start_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="startMm")
    end_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="endMm")
    force_pin_override: bool = Field(default=False, alias="forcePinOverride")

content.content:
cmd_raw: dict[str, Any],
    *,
    source_provider: SourceDocProvider | None = None,
) -> tuple[bool, Document | None, Command, list[Violation], str]:
    cmds = coerce_command(cmd_raw)
    cand = clone_document(doc)
    # KRN-06: backfill the singleton on every commit so persisted state always has it.
    ensure_internal_origin(cand)
    apply_inplace(cand, cmds, source_provider=source_provider)
    ensure_sun_settings(cand)

    violations = evaluate(cand.elements)

    blocking = [v for v in violations if v.blocking or v.severity == "error"]
    if blocking:
        return False, None, cmds, violations, "constraint_error"

    cand.revision = doc.revision + 1

    return True, cand, cmds, violations, "ok"

def resolve_stack_wall_type_at_cut(wall: WallElem, cut_plane_offset_mm: float) -> str | None:
    """Return the wallTypeId of the component containing the cut-plane height.

    Falls back to wall.wall_type_id for non-stacked walls.
    ``cut_plane_offset_mm`` is measured from the wall's base (level elevation).
    """
    if wall.stack is None or not wall.stack.components:
        return wall.wall_type_id
    accumulated = 0.0
    for i, component in enumerate(wall.stack.components):
        is_last = i == len(wall.stack.components) - 1
        top = wall.height_mm if is_last else accumulated + component.height_mm
        if accumulated <= cut_plane_offset_mm < top:
            return component.wall_type_id
        accumulated += component.height_mm
    return wall.stack.components[-1].wall_type_id


def schedule_stacked_components(wall: WallElem) -> list[dict]:
    """Enumerate stack components as schedule rows.

    Returns one row per component (or one row for a non-stacked wall).
    Keys: wallId, wallTypeId, heightMm, componentIndex.
    The last component's heightMm is the remainder (wall.height_mm - prefix sum).
    """
    if wall.stack is None or not wall.stack.components:
        return [
            {
                "wallId": wall.id,
                "wallTypeId": wall.wall_type_id,
                "heightMm": wall.height_mm,
                "componentIndex": 0,
            }
        ]
    rows = []
    accumulated = 0.0
    for i, comp in enumerate(wall.stack.components):
        is_last = i == len(wall.stack.components) - 1
        effective_height = (wall.height_mm - accumulated) if is_last else comp.height_mm
        rows.append(
            {
                "wallId": wall.id,
                "wallTypeId": comp.wall_type_id,
                "heightMm": effective_height,
                "componentIndex": i,
            }
        )
        accumulated += comp.height_mm
    return rows

if cmd.shape == "sketch" and not runs:
        assert cmd.sketch_path_mm is not None and len(cmd.sketch_path_mm) >= 2
        polyline = list(cmd.sketch_path_mm)

        runs = [

                id=f"{cmd.id or 'stair'}-run-1",

        for i in range(len(cmd.tread_lines) - 1):

            mid_a = Vec2Mm(

                    start_mm=mid_a,

                    width_mm=cmd.width_mm,
                    riser_count=1,

            )

    return runs, landings

input.new_string:
def _emit_stair_by_sketch(session: SketchSession, opts: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """KRN-V3-05 — emit a CreateStair with authoringMode='by_sketch' from opts."""

    from bim_ai.sketch_validation import SketchInvalidError

    base_level_id = opts.get("baseLevelId") or session.level_id
    top_level_id = opts.get("topLevelId")
    if not top_level_id:
        raise SketchInvalidError(
            "missing_top_level",
            "stair_by_sketch Finish requires `topLevelId` in options.",
        )

    boundary_mm = opts.get("boundaryMm")
    if not boundary_mm or len(boundary_mm) < 3:
        raise SketchInvalidError(
            "missing_boundary",
            "stair_by_sketch Finish requires `boundaryMm` with ≥ 3 points.",
        )

    tread_lines = opts.get("treadLines")
    if not tread_lines or len(tread_lines) < 1:
        raise SketchInvalidError(
            "missing_tread_lines",
            "stair_by_sketch Finish requires `treadLines` with ≥ 1 entry.",
        )

    total_rise_mm = opts.get("totalRiseMm")
    if not total_rise_mm or total_rise_mm <= 0:
        raise SketchInvalidError(
            "missing_total_rise",
            "stair_by_sketch Finish requires `totalRiseMm` > 0.",
        )

    cmd: dict[str, Any] = {
        "type": "createStair",
        "name": opts.get("name", "Stair"),
        "baseLevelId": base_level_id,
        "topLevelId": top_level_id,
        "runStartMm": boundary_mm[0],
        "runEndMm": boundary_mm[-1],
        "authoringMode": "by_sketch",
        "boundaryMm": boundary_mm,
        "treadLines": tread_lines,
        "totalRiseMm": total_rise_mm,
    }
    return [cmd]


SUBMODES: dict[str, SubmodeSpec] = {
    "floor": SubmodeSpec(_validate_polygon_session, _emit_floor),
    "roof": SubmodeSpec(_validate_polygon_session, _emit_roof),
    "room_separation": SubmodeSpec(_validate_line_set_session, _emit_room_separation),
    "ceiling": SubmodeSpec(_validate_polygon_session, _emit_ceiling),
    "in_place_mass": SubmodeSpec(_validate_polygon_session, _emit_in_place_mass),
    "void_cut": SubmodeSpec(_validate_polygon_session, _emit_void_cut),
    "detail_region": SubmodeSpec(_validate_line_set_session, _emit_detail_region),
    "stair": SubmodeSpec(_validate_line_set_session, _emit_stair),
    "stair_by_sketch": SubmodeSpec(_validate_line_set_session, _emit_stair_by_sketch),
}

def _emit_stair_by_sketch(session: SketchSession, opts: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """KRN-V3-05 — emit a CreateStair with authoringMode='by_sketch' from opts."""

    from bim_ai.sketch_validation import SketchInvalidError

    base_level_id = opts.get("baseLevelId") or session.level_id
    top_level_id = opts.get("topLevelId")
    if not top_level_id:
        raise SketchInvalidError(
            "missing_top_level",
            "stair_by_sketch Finish requires `topLevelId` in options.",
        )

    boundary_mm = opts.get("boundaryMm")
    if not boundary_mm or len(boundary_mm) < 3:
        raise SketchInvalidError(
            "missing_boundary",
            "stair_by_sketch Finish requires `boundaryMm` with ≥ 3 points.",
        )

    tread_lines = opts.get("treadLines")
    if not tread_lines or len(tread_lines) < 1:
        raise SketchInvalidError(
            "missing_tread_lines",
            "stair_by_sketch Finish requires `treadLines` with ≥ 1 entry.",
        )

    total_rise_mm = opts.get("totalRiseMm")
    if not total_rise_mm or total_rise_mm <= 0:
        raise SketchInvalidError(
            "missing_total_rise",
            "stair_by_sketch Finish requires `totalRiseMm` > 0.",
        )

    cmd: dict[str, Any] = {
        "type": "createStair",
        "name": opts.get("name", "Stair"),
        "baseLevelId": base_level_id,
        "topLevelId": top_level_id,
        "runStartMm": boundary_mm[0],
        "runEndMm": boundary_mm[-1],
        "authoringMode": "by_sketch",
        "boundaryMm": boundary_mm,
        "treadLines": tread_lines,
        "totalRiseMm": total_rise_mm,
    }
    return [cmd]

def _validate_polygon_session(session: SketchSession) -> None:
    """Validate a polygon-style sub-mode (floor / roof / ceiling / mass / void).

    Two layers, both required:

    1. :func:`validate_session` does the structural check (vertex incidence,
       zero-length lines, self-intersection) on the original ``SketchLine``
       list. This catches the most common authoring mistakes ("I forgot the
       last segment").
    2. :func:`assert_closed_loop` is the lightweight guard on the derived
       polygon — degenerate consecutive vertices land here.

    Imports happen inside the function to avoid a circular import between
    `sketch_session` and `sketch_validation` (the latter imports from this
    module at top level).
    """

    from bim_ai.sketch_validation import (
        SketchInvalidError,
        assert_closed_loop,
        derive_closed_loop_polygon,
        validate_session,
    )

    state = validate_session(list(session.lines))
    if not state.valid and state.issues:
        first = state.issues[0]
        raise SketchInvalidError(first.code, first.message)

    polygon_xy = derive_closed_loop_polygon(list(session.lines))
    polygon = [Vec2Mm(xMm=x, yMm=y) for (x, y) in polygon_xy]
    assert_closed_loop(polygon)


def _validate_line_set_session(session: SketchSession) -> None:
    """Validate a line-set sub-mode (room_separation / detail_region)."""

    from bim_ai.sketch_validation import assert_line_set

    segments = [(ln.from_mm, ln.to_mm) for ln in session.lines]

input.new_string:
def _validate_stair_boundary(points: list[Vec2Mm]) -> None:
    """Raise ValueError for invalid stair boundary polygons."""
    if len(points) < 3:
        raise ValueError("stair boundary must have ≥ 3 points")
    n = len(points)
    # Self-intersection check runs before area so bowtie shapes get the right error.
    for i in range(n):
        ax, ay = points[i].x_mm, points[i].y_mm
        bx, by = points[(i + 1) % n].x_mm, points[(i + 1) % n].y_mm
        for j in range(i + 2, n):
            if i == 0 and j == n - 1:
                continue
            cx, cy = points[j].x_mm, points[j].y_mm
            dx, dy = points[(j + 1) % n].x_mm, points[(j + 1) % n].y_mm
            rx, ry = bx - ax, by - ay
            sx, sy = dx - cx, dy - cy
            denom = rx * sy - ry * sx
            if abs(denom) < 1e-7:
                continue
            qpx, qpy = cx - ax, cy - ay
            t = (qpx * sy - qpy * sx) / denom
            u = (qpx * ry - qpy * rx) / denom
            eps = 1e-6
            if (eps < t < 1.0 - eps) and (eps < u < 1.0 - eps):
                raise ValueError(
                    f"stair boundary is self-intersecting (edge {i} crosses edge {j})"
                )
    area2 = 0.0
    for i in range(n):
        a = points[i]
        b = points[(i + 1) % n]
        area2 += a.x_mm * b.y_mm - b.x_mm * a.y_mm
    if abs(area2) < 1.0:
        raise ValueError("stair boundary has zero area")

- WallStackComponent + WallStack models in elements.py
- WallStackComponentCmd + SetWallStackCmd in commands.py + Command union
- CreateWallCmd.stackComponents optional field
- _validate_wall_stack helper + CreateWallCmd/SetWallStackCmd handlers in engine.py

WP-012 / KRN-V3-05:
- StairTreadLine + StairElem.authoringMode/boundaryMm/treadLines/totalRiseMm (already in elements.py)
- CreateStairCmd extended; _balance_tread_risers + _validate_stair_boundary already in engine.py
- _materialize_stair_runs_and_landings: by_sketch branch (one run per tread cell)

- sketch_session.py: stair_by_sketch submode + _emit_stair_by_sketch + SketchElementKind extended
- sketchApi.ts: stair_by_sketch kind + StairSketchFinishOpts type + options param on finishSketchSession
- StairTreadLine TS type + stair by_sketch fields in index.ts
- StairBySketchCanvas.tsx: two-phase boundary+tread drawing overlay with EU comfort advisory
- 17 tests in test_stair_by_sketch.py — all pass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
All done. Here's the status:

**WP-010 (KRN-V3-02 stacked walls) — implemented:**
- `WallStackComponent` + `WallStack` models in elements.py + TS types
- `WallStackComponentCmd` + `SetWallStackCmd` in commands.py
- `CreateWallCmd.stackComponents` optional field
- `_validate_wall_stack`, `CreateWallCmd`/`SetWallStackCmd` handlers in engine.py
- `resolve_stack_wall_type_at_cut` + `schedule_stacked_components` pure helpers
- 12/12 tests pass

**WP-012 (KRN-V3-05 stair by sketch) — implemented:**
- `_materialize_stair_runs_and_landings` extended with by_sketch branch (one run per tread cell pair)
- `CreateStairCmd` dispatch passes `authoring_mode`, `boundary_mm`, balanced `tread_lines`, `total_rise_mm`
- `stair_by_sketch` submode added to sketch_session.py
- `sketchApi.ts`: `stair_by_sketch` kind + `StairSketchFinishOpts` + `options` param on `finishSketchSession`
- TS types: `StairTreadLine`, stair by_sketch fields on elem
- `StairBySketchCanvas.tsx`: two-phase boundary+tread overlay with EU comfort advisory
- 17/17 tests pass

**Wave-3 is now unblocked with 8 free slots.** The next batch to dispatch: KRN-V3-07 (slanted walls), KRN-V3-08 (wall sweeps), KRN-V3-10 (stair sub-kinds), KRN-V3-11 (railing), CMD-V3-02 (assumption log), COL-V3-01 (multi-user), JOB-V3-01 (jobs panel), plus one chrome WP.

content: All 15 v3 work packages are now merged into main, including WP-010 (stacked walls) and WP-012 (stair by sketch) which were implemented directly by the orchestrator. Next: spawn up to 8 new worker sessions using the wave-3 candidates listed in spec/v3-build-state.md.

input.description: Write wave-3 WP prompt files (wp-016 through wp-023)
input.prompt:
Read `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` and find the full entries for these 8 work packages:
- KRN-V3-07 slanted/tapered walls
- KRN-V3-08 wall sweeps & reveals
- KRN-V3-10 monolithic/floating stair sub-kinds
- KRN-V3-11 railing/baluster
- CMD-V3-02 assumption log
- COL-V3-01 multi-user collab
- JOB-V3-01 Jobs panel
- CHR-V3-03 status bar (or CHR-V3-04 Cmd+K palette — pick whichever appears first in the tracker)

Then write 8 prompt files at `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/v3-prompts/wp-016.md` through `wp-023.md` following the exact same format as the existing wave-2 prompts. Use wp-009.md, wp-010.md, and wp-012.md as templates for the format.

Each prompt file must contain:
1. A header with WP-NNN, WP-ID, branch name, effort, and state=now
2. Required reading list (key files the implementor needs to read first)
3. The verbatim WP entry from the tracker
4. Setup bash commands (git fetch, checkout main, pull, create branch)
5. Numbered implementation tasks with exact code snippets wherever the tracker specifies data models, function signatures, or algorithms
6. Implementation rules section (API-V3-01 contract, no hex literals, pydantic discipline, etc.)
7. Verify gate (pytest commands, make verify, pnpm tsc --noEmit)
8. Commit and push commands
9. Final report template

Number the WPs as follows:
- wp-016.md = KRN-V3-07
- wp-017.md = KRN-V3-08
- wp-018.md = KRN-V3-10
- wp-019.md = KRN-V3-11
- wp-020.md = CMD-V3-02
- wp-021.md = COL-V3-01
- wp-022.md = JOB-V3-01
- wp-023.md = CHR-V3-03 (or CHR-V3-04 whichever is in tracker)

Context: The following are already merged on main so implementors should read them as reference:
- KRN-V3-02 stacked walls (elements.py: WallStackComponent, WallStack; engine.py: SetWallStackCmd handler)
- KRN-V3-03 multi-roof + soffit (engine.py: RoofJoinElem, EdgeProfileRunElem, SoffitElem)
- KRN-V3-05 stair by sketch (elements.py: StairTreadLine, StairElem.authoring_mode/boundary_mm; engine.py: _balance_tread_risers, _validate_stair_boundary)
- CMD-V3-01 apply-bundle (app/bim_ai/cmd/apply_bundle.py: CommandBundle, BundleResult, AssumptionEntry)
- CQ-01 WebSocket robustness (hub.py: resume(), ring buffer; useWorkspaceSnapshot.ts: reconnect logic)

The repo root is `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`. Write all 8 files. Return a summary of what was written.

content.text:
All 8 files are in place. Here is a summary of what was written:

---

All 8 prompt files written to `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/v3-prompts/`:

**wp-016.md — KRN-V3-07: Slanted & Tapered Walls** (13 KB)
Branch `feat/v3-krn-v3-07-slanted-tapered-walls`. Adds `leanMm` + `taperRatio` to `WallElem`; `SetWallLeanTaperCmd`; `_validate_wall_lean_taper` (lean ≤ h×tan 60°, taper ∈ (0.1,10)); `resolve_wall_face_offset_at_cut` pure helper; extends `CreateWallCmd`. Notes the KRN-V3-02 merge to avoid conflict in `apply_inplace`.

**wp-017.md — KRN-V3-08: Wall Sweeps & Reveals** (12 KB)
Branch `feat/v3-krn-v3-08-wall-sweeps-reveals`. Extends `EdgeProfileRunElem` (KRN-V3-03) with `mode: 'sweep'|'reveal'` and `WallEdgeSpec` host variants; `SetEdgeProfileRunModeCmd`; wall-host branch in `CreateEdgeProfileRunCmd`; `compute_wall_corner_mitre_angle` pure helper. Explicitly avoids duplicating KRN-V3-03 work.

**wp-018.md — KRN-V3-10: Monolithic / Floating Stair Sub-kinds** (13 KB)
Branch `feat/v3-krn-v3-10-stair-subkinds`. Adds `subKind`, `monolithicMaterial`, `floatingTreadDepthMm`, `floatingHostWallId` to `StairElem`; `SetStairSubKindCmd`; `_validate_stair_sub_kind` enforces floating-requires-host-wall. Notes KRN-V3-05 field isolation.

**wp-019.md — KRN-V3-11: Railing Baluster Pattern + Handrail Supports** (13 KB)
Branch `feat/v3-krn-v3-11-railing-baluster`. `BalusterPattern` + `HandrailSupport` models; extends `RailingElem`; `SetRailingBalusterPatternCmd` + `SetRailingHandrailSupportsCmd`; `_validate_baluster_pattern` (regular requires spacingMm); `_validate_handrail_supports` (hostWallId must be WallElem); `compute_baluster_positions` pure helper.

**wp-020.md — CMD-V3-02: Assumption Log Primitive** (14 KB)
Branch `feat/v3-cmd-v3-02-assumption-log`. Builds on CMD-V3-01 (WP-009): adds `AgentTrace` model; `agent_trace: AgentTrace | None` field on kernel elements; `_attach_agent_traces` stamps new/modified elements on commit; `_write_assumption_audit` best-effort JSONL audit log; `AssumptionLogSection.tsx` Inspector skeleton; ToolDescriptor `outputSchema` update.

**wp-021.md — COL-V3-01: Multi-user Editing Infra (yjs / CRDT)** (18 KB)
Branch `feat/v3-col-v3-01-multiuser-yjs`. XL WP. Covers: CQ-01 hard-prerequisite check; `CollabOrchestrator` + `CollabRoom` Python module; yjs Y-WebSocket route `/api/models/{model_id}/collab`; `CollabProvider.tsx` + `ParticipantStrip.tsx` React components; remote-selection halo extension; architecture note that kernel commit boundary (try_commit_bundle) is unchanged.

**wp-022.md — JOB-V3-01: Long-running-operations Jobs UX** (19 KB)
Branch `feat/v3-job-v3-01-jobs-panel`. CQ-01 hard-prerequisite check; `app/bim_ai/jobs/` module with `Job` Pydantic model + `JobQueue` singleton; 5 REST endpoints (create/list/get/cancel/retry); WS broadcast via hub; `JobsPanel.tsx` (320 px, `J` shortcut, live updates); ToolDescriptor + CLI `bim-ai jobs submit/list/cancel/status`; single-concurrency v3.0 with pluggable interface.

**wp-023.md — CHR-V3-03: Status Bar with Lens Dropdown** (11 KB)
Branch `feat/v3-chr-v3-03-status-bar`. Pure frontend WP. 6-slot 28 px bar (`--color-surface-strong`, `--radius-canvas: 0`); `LensDropdown` slot 5 as A8 antidote (replaces filter dialog); ghost opacity 240 ms `--ease-paper`; `DriftBadge` slot 6 with `--color-drift` token; keyboard cycling; Vitest + ESLint no-hex gate.

Every file follows the exact same section structure as the wave-2 templates (required reading, verbatim WP entry, setup bash, numbered tasks with code snippets, implementation rules, verify gate, commit/push, final report template) and cross-references the already-merged WPs as reading material where relevant.
